title: Smoky Creamy Indulgent Moroccan Lamb Tangia
description: A rich and aromatic Moroccan lamb dish cooked in a sweet and smoky syrup, served in a delicate lacquerware bowl.
instructions: Season the lamb shoulder with salt, black pepper, cinnamon, cumin, paprika, and smoked paprika.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Sear the lamb shoulder until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes. Transfer the lamb to a large oven-safe pot or Dutch oven.
Sauté the onion, carrot, and celery in the same skillet until the vegetables are tender, about 5 minutes. Add the garlic and ginger and cook for 1 minute more.
Add the sugar syrup, lemon juice, and honey to the skillet and stir to combine. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes.
Add the browned lamb shoulder to the pot and cover it with a lid.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and braise the lamb for 2 1/2 hours, or until tender and falling apart.
Remove the pot from the oven and sprinkle the lamb with fresh parsley and cilantro. Serve hot in a delicate lacquerware bowl.
Garnish with additional fresh herbs, if desired.
Let the lamb rest for 10-15 minutes before slicing and serving.
Slice the lamb against the grain and serve with the rich and aromatic sauce spooned over the top.
Serve with steamed vegetables or a side of couscous, if desired.
